# Team News: Argentina vs. Austria injury, suspension list, predicted XIs | World Cup 2026

By Lewis Nolan| 21 Jun 2026 18:00

© Iconsport / ZUMA Press Wire

Argentina will look to qualify for the 2026 World Cup's round of 32 when they face Austria on Monday at Dallas Stadium.

Lionel Messi guided his Argentine teammates to a comfortable 3-0 victory over Algeria on Wednesday, and they come into their clash with Austria in first place, though their opponents are second with three points.

Here, Sports Mole rounds up the team news for both nations.

---

## ARGENTINA vs. AUSTRIA

https://www.sportsmole.co.uk/football/argentina/world-cup-2026/preview/argentina-vs-austria-prediction-team-news-lineups_599671.html

## ARGENTINA

Out: None

Doubtful: Gonzalo Montiel(hamstring)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: E Martinez; Molina, Romero, Li Martinez, Medina; De Paul, Mac Allister, Fernandez, Almada; Messi, La Martinez

## AUSTRIA

Out: None

Doubtful: Stefan Posch(broken jaw), David Alaba(muscle fatigue), Alessandro Schopf(fitne

news_items.title: Argentina XI vs Austria: Predicted lineup, confirmed team news, injury latest for World Cup today - Yahoo Sports
news_items.author: Tashan Deniran-Alleyne Mon, June 22, 2026 at 9:00 a.m. UTC · 2 min read
news_items.text:
Argentina XI vs Austria: Predicted lineup, confirmed team news, injury latest for World Cup today - Yahoo Sports

Advertisement

Advertisement

Advertisement

Advertisement

Story by

Argentina XI vs Austria: Predicted lineup, confirmed team news, injury latest for World Cup today

Lionel Messi will look to become the World Cup’s all-time leading scorer when Argentina face Austria today.

A hat-trick against Algeria in the Group J opener in Kansas City last week took the football icon level with record holder Miroslav Klose of Germany on 16 finals goals.

Advertisement

Advertisement

Messi - who turns 39 on Wednesday - will look to make history when the World Cup holders meet Ralf Rangnick’s Austria in Dallas on Monday.

Argentina head coach Lionel Scaloni will be forced into at least one change for the match.

Gonzalo Montiel, scorer of the winning penalty in the World Cup final against France in 2022, has been ruled out of the second group stage game through injury.

The right-back was forced off at half-time of the 3-0 win over Algeria with a hamstring issue and while it is not thought to be too serious, he is not expected to be risked in Texas.

Advertisement

Advertisement

A

Argentina XI vs Austria: Predicted lineup, confirmed team news, injury latest for World Cup today

Lionel Messi will look to become the World Cup’s all-time leading scorer when Argentina face Austria today.

A hat-trick against Algeria in the Group J opener in Kansas City last week took the football icon level with record holder Miroslav Klose of Germany on 16 finals goals.

Advertisement

Advertisement

Messi - who turns 39 on Wednesday - will look to make history when the World Cup holders meet Ralf Rangnick’s Austria in Dallas on Monday.

Argentina head coach Lionel Scaloni will be forced into at least one change for the match.

Gonzalo Montiel, scorer of the winning penalty in the World Cup final against France in 2022, has been ruled out of the second group stage game through injury.

The right-back was forced off at half-time of the 3-0 win over Algeria with a hamstring issue and while it is not thought to be too serious, he is not expected to be risked in Texas.

# Argentina vs. Austria projected lineups, starting 11 for World Cup 2026 Group J game in Dallas

Dom Farrell

•

SN

Add The Sporting News on Google

Argentina and Austria each got off to winning starts in World Cup Group J and meet at AT&T Stadium on Monday.

The defending champions dazzled on their opening night, with Lionel Messi typically the star of the show as he scored a hat-trick in a 3-0 win over Algeria.

One more goal will see Messi go ahead of Miroslav Klose at the top of the World Cup's all-time scoring charts.

Updated on June 22, 2026 4:15AM EST

Pierre Courtin

Adam Zdroik

Updated on June 22, 2026 4:15AM EST

# Argentina vs Austria Preview: Predicted Lineups, Team News & Tactical Analysis | 2026 World Cup Group J

Group J's heavyweight tie arrives in the second round at AT&T Stadium in Arlington. Argentina opened their title defense with a Lionel Messi hat-trick in a 3-0 win over Algeria, while Austria, back at a World Cup for the first time since 1998, came from behind to beat Jordan 3-1. Both sit on three points, so this is effectively a play-in for top spot and a big step toward the round of 32.

Argentina are heavy favorites, and given the

# Team News: Argentina vs. Austria injury, suspension list, predicted XIs | World Cup 2026

By Lewis Nolan| 21 Jun 2026 18:00

© Iconsport / ZUMA Press Wire

Argentina will look to qualify for the 2026 World Cup's round of 32 when they face Austria on Monday at Dallas Stadium.

Lionel Messi guided his Argentine teammates to a comfortable 3-0 victory over Algeria on Wednesday, and they come into their clash with Austria in first place, though their opponents are second with three points.

Here, Sports Mole rounds up the team news for both nations.

---

## ARGENTINA vs. AUSTRIA

https://www.sportsmole.co.uk/football/argentina/world-cup-2026/preview/argentina-vs-austria-prediction-team-news-lineups_599671.html

## ARGENTINA

Out: None

Doubtful: Gonzalo Montiel(hamstring)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: E Martinez; Molina, Romero, Li Martinez, Medina; De Paul, Mac Allister, Fernandez, Almada; Messi, La Martinez

Out: None

Argentina XI vs Austria: Predicted lineup, confirmed team news, injury latest for World Cup today

Lionel Messi will look to become the World Cup’s all-time leading scorer when Argentina face Austria today.

A hat-trick against Algeria in the Group J opener in Kansas City last week took the football icon level with record holder Miroslav Klose of Germany on 16 finals goals.

Advertisement

Advertisement

Messi - who turns 39 on Wednesday - will look to make history when the World Cup holders meet Ralf Rangnick’s Austria in Dallas on Monday.

Argentina head coach Lionel Scaloni will be forced into at least one change for the match.

Gonzalo Montiel, scorer of the winning penalty in the World Cup final against France in 2022, has been ruled out of the second group stage game through injury.

The right-back was forced off at half-time of the 3-0 win over Algeria with a hamstring issue and while it is not thought to be too serious, he is not expected to be risked in Texas.

# Argentina vs. Austria projected lineups, starting 11 for World Cup 2026 Group J game in Dallas

Dom Farrell

•

SN

Add The Sporting News on Google

Argentina and Austria each got off to winning starts in World Cup Group J and meet at AT&T Stadium on Monday.

The defending champions dazzled on their opening night, with Lionel Messi typically the star of the show as he scored a hat-trick in a 3-0 win over Algeria.

One more goal will see Messi go ahead of Miroslav Klose at the top of the World Cup's all-time scoring charts.

# Argentina vs Austria Preview: Predicted Lineups, Team News & Tactical Analysis | 2026 World Cup Group J

Group J's heavyweight tie arrives in the second round at AT&T Stadium in Arlington. Argentina opened their title defense with a Lionel Messi hat-trick in a 3-0 win over Algeria, while Austria, back at a World Cup for the first time since 1998, came from behind to beat Jordan 3-1.
